data.text: Now let me read the perf test files to understand their structure:

data.output:
1	// Package perf provides a reusable GraphQL performance harness for the DDx
2	// server. It seeds realistic on-disk fixtures (projects × beads, docgraph,
3	// agent sessions) using the real storage primitives, then drives each GraphQL
4	// query shape through both the in-process StateProvider and the full HTTP
5	// handler while recording p50/p95/p99 latencies.
6	//
7	// The harness exists so performance work can be grounded in numbers rather
8	// than anecdote (ddx-9ce6842a Part 1). New query shapes are added by
9	// appending to the target table in harness.go — the fixture and measurement
10	// layers stay unchanged.

1	package perf
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"testing"
6		"time"
7	)
8

10	// side). Opening the per-project `/beads` page results in a

5	// raceEnabled reports whether the test binary was built with `-race`.

7	// adds 5-10x synchronisation overhead that invalidates microbenchmark
8	// percentile gates. Build-tag pair lives in race_off_test.go (false) and
9	// race_on_test.go (true) so callers see one constant either way.
10	const raceEnabled = false

- name: Run frontend Playwright E2E tests

  # Perf tests — non-gating; run weekly and on manual dispatch, or on PRs touching the perf package
  perf:
    name: Perf Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: |
      github.event_name == 'schedule' ||
      github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' ||
      (github.event_name == 'pull_request' && contains(github.event.pull_request.changed_files, 'cli/internal/server/perf/'))
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: cli
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Set up Go
      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
      with:
        go-version-file: 'cli/go.mod'
        cache-dependency-path: cli/go.sum

    - name: Run perf tests
      run: make test-perf

data.text: The `contains(github.event.pull_request.changed_files, ...)` condition isn't valid GitHub Actions syntax. Let me simplify the condition to just schedule and manual dispatch (which matches the bead intent of "non-gating"):

input.old_string:
# Perf tests — non-gating; run weekly and on manual dispatch, or on PRs touching the perf package
  perf:
    name: Perf Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: |
      github.event_name == 'schedule' ||
      github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' ||
      (github.event_name == 'pull_request' && contains(github.event.pull_request.changed_files, 'cli/internal/server/perf/'))
input.new_string:
# Perf tests — non-gating; run weekly on schedule or on manual dispatch only
  perf:
    name: Perf Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'schedule' || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'

data.text:
All 7 ACs satisfied:

1. Every `*_test.go` in `cli/internal/server/perf/` has `//go:build perf` (or `//go:build perf && !race` / `//go:build perf && race` for the race pair).
2. `go test ./...` reports `[no test files]` for the perf package — zero perf tests run.
3. `go test -tags=perf ./internal/server/perf/...` will run the tests (tag gates them in).
4. Makefile has `test-perf` target with `-tags=perf` and 5m timeout.
5. `.github/workflows/ci.yml` has a `perf` job triggered on weekly schedule and manual dispatch.
6. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
